item.title: Trump authorizes direct US flights to Lebanon after 4-decade halt
item.description: Direct flights between the United States and Lebanon have not been operational since 1985.

item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: Iran targeted US radar and air defence installations in the Gulf on Tuesday, warning Washington of "more decisive and powerful" attacks to come as the conflict threatened to widen with a blockade of Saudi ports by Houthi rebels in Yemen. Trump warns Houthis Trump said he would "take care of" the Houthi rebels if the Iran-backed militant group follows through on its threatened blockade of Saudi ports.

item.title: PJAK leader mocks Iran’s claim Turkey spiked US Kurdish-backed regime change plan
item.description: The PJAK co-leader says he does not believe Washington or Israel would abandon a strategic objective as significant as toppling Iran's government because of Turkish objections.

item.title: PJAK leader mocks Iran’s claim Turkey spiked US Kurdish-backed regime change plan
item.description: The PJAK co-leader says he does not believe Washington or Israel would abandon a strategic objective as significant as toppling Iran's government because of Turkish objections.

item.title: PJAK leader mocks Iran’s claim Turkey spiked US Kurdish-backed regime change plan
item.description: The PJAK co-leader says he does not believe Washington or Israel would abandon a strategic objective as significant as toppling Iran's government because of Turkish objections.

item.title: PJAK leader mocks Iran’s claim Turkey spiked US Kurdish-backed regime change plan
item.description: The PJAK co-leader says he does not believe Washington or Israel would abandon a strategic objective as significant as toppling Iran's government because of Turkish objections.

item.title: Syria arrivals surge 111% as government bets on tourism revival
item.description: The country welcomed 3.52 million visitors during the first half of 2026, more than double the figure recorded a year earlier, as the government pushes to reconnect to the world and promote tourism as a pillar of economic recovery.

item.title: Syria arrivals surge 111% as government bets on tourism revival
item.description: The country welcomed 3.52 million visitors during the first half of 2026, more than double the figure recorded a year earlier, as the government pushes to reconnect to the world and promote tourism as a pillar of economic recovery.

item.description: The country welcomed 3.52 million visitors during the first half of 2026, more than double the figure recorded a year earlier, as the government pushes to reconnect to the world and promote tourism as a pillar of economic recovery.

item.title: Syria arrivals surge 111% as government bets on tourism revival
item.description: The country welcomed 3.52 million visitors during the first half of 2026, more than double the figure recorded a year earlier, as the government pushes to reconnect to the world and promote tourism as a pillar of economic recovery.

item.title: Explainer-When are Turkey's next elections, and can Erdogan run again?
item.description:
By Huseyin Hayatsever

ANKARA, July 21 (Reuters) - Turkey's opposition leader, Ozgur Ozel, said he would form a new party to challenge Tayyip Erdogan, a political splintering that could boost the president's prospects of extending his 23-year rule at the next elections.

item.title: 'Interaction' with Iran's injured Supreme Leader increasing daily, president says
item.description:
DUBAI, July 21 (Reuters) - The level of "interaction" with Iran's Supreme Leader is increasing day by day, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said in a video clip published on state media on Tuesday.

His remarks follow months of speculation about the health of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who has not been seen in public since the airstrike that killed his father and predecessor, Ali Khamenei, at the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Turkey's opposition leader to form new party to challenge Erdogan
item.description:
By Huseyin Hayatsever

ANKARA, July 21 (Reuters) - Turkish opposition leader Ozgur Ozel said on Tuesday he would resign his post and form a new party, a political splintering that compounds the challenges facing President Tayyip Erdogan's opponents as they face an unprecedented legal crackdown.

The new party is expected to spark an exodus of voters and parliamentarians from the secularist Republican People's Party (CHP), which modern Turkey's founder Mustafa Kemal Ataturk established more than a century ago.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Kuwaitis ration electricity as Iran hits power grid
item.description: Residents of Kuwait, one of the world's hottest countries, are cutting their use of air conditioning at the height of summer, as Iranian strikes on power plants turn rationing into a national priority. Energy-guzzling air conditioners are ubiquitous in Kuwait during the summer months.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping companies to avoid Saudi ports, email shows
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul and Renee Maltezou

LONDON/ATHENS, July 21 (Reuters) - Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthi militia told shipping companies not to load or discharge cargo at Saudi Arabian ports or they may be targeted, according to an email the group sent to companies.

The Houthis on Monday declared a naval blockade against Saudi Arabia, opening a potential new front against the United States in its war with Iran and raising the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf.

item.title: Trump 'not finished' with Iran as Tehran steps up strikes
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that the United States was not finished attacking Iran, as Tehran stepped up its strikes across the Middle East. As Iran stepped up its attacks in the Gulf, Kuwait's foreign ministry on Tuesday said it had summoned Iran's ambassador over a strike against a ship passing through the Hormuz strait the day before.
